mae_clauses.text: Material Adverse Effect" means any event, change, circumstance, occurrence, effect or state of facts (each, an "Effect") that, individually or in the aggregate with all other Effects, (a) has been or would reasonably be expected to be materially adverse to the business, results of operations or financial condition of the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, or Parent and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, as applicable, or (b) would reasonably be expected to, individually or in the aggregate, impair in any material respect the ability of the Company or Parent, as applicable, to perform its obligations under this Agreement or to consummate the Merger, or prevent or materially delay the consummation of any of the Merger and the other transactions contemplated by this Agreement; provided, however, that, in the case of clause (a) only, in no event shall any of the following, alone or in combination, be deemed to constitute, or be taken into account in determining whether there has been or will be, a Material Adverse Effect: (i) any change in general United States or global economic conditions, including changes in the credit, debt, financial or capital markets (including changes in interest or exchange rates), in each case in the United States or anywhere else in the world, (ii) any change that generally affects any of the industries or markets in which the Company and its Subsidiaries, or Parent and its Subsidiaries, as applicable, operate, (iii) any change in political conditions (or the escalation of any such change in political conditions existing as of the date of this Agreement) or any hostilities, war, police action, acts of terrorism or natural disasters (or the escalation of any such hostilities, war, police action, acts of terrorism or natural disasters existing as of the date of this Agreement) in the United States or anywhere else in the world, (iv) any change in applicable Law or GAAP or other applicable accounting standards (or the interpretation thereof), (v) any change arising from or relating to the announcement or pendency of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement, including the impact thereof on relationships, contractual or otherwise, with customers, suppliers, licensors, distributors, partners, providers and employees (provided that the exception in this clause (v) shall not apply to the representation and warranty set forth in Section 3.5(a)(iii) or Section 4.5(a)(iii), as applicable, or for purposes of determining whether the condition set forth in Section 6.2(a) or Section 6.3(a), as applicable, has been satisfied with respect to such representation and warranty), (vi) any failure by the Company or Parent, as applicable, to meet any projections, forecasts, guidance, estimates, milestones, budgets or financial or operating predictions of revenue, earnings, cash flow or cash position (provided that the exception in this clause (vi) shall not prevent a determination that any Effect underlying such failure to meet projections, forecasts, guidance, estimates, milestones, budgets or financial or operating predictions has resulted in a Material Adverse Effect (to the extent such Effect is not otherwise excluded from this definition of Material Adverse Effect)), (vii) any Effect arising from or relating to the taking of any action contemplated or permitted by this Agreement or taken at the written request of Parent or the Company, as applicable, (viii) any hurricane, tornado, flood, earthquake, tsunami, other natural disaster, pandemic (including COVID-19 or any COVID-19 Measures), epidemic, disease outbreak or other force majeure event in the United States or anywhere else in the world or (ix) any change in the Company's stock price or the trading volume of the Company's stock, or Parent's share price or the trading volume of Parent Shares, as applicable, or any change in the credit rating of the Company or Parent, as applicable (provided that the exception in this clause (ix) shall not prevent a determination that any Effect underlying such change in stock or share price, trading volume or credit rating has resulted in a Material Adverse Effect (to the extent such Effect is not otherwise excluded from this definition of Material Adverse Effect)); provided further, however, that any Effect referred to in clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (iv) or (viii) immediately above shall be taken into account in determining whether a Material Adverse Effect has occurred or would reasonably be expected to occur to the extent that such Effect has a disproportionate effect on the Company and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, or Parent and its Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, as applicable, compared to other participants in the industries in which the Company and its Subsidiaries, or Parent and its Subsidiaries, as applicable, conduct their businesses (in which case only the incremental disproportionate impact may be taken into account in determining whether there has been or will be a Material Adverse Effect).
